#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk {background-color: #FFFFFF;}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-policy-title,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-policy-text,
#onetrust-consent-sdk .ot-b-addl-desc,
#onetrust-consent-sdk .ot-dpd-desc,
#onetrust-consent-sdk .ot-dpd-title,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-policy-text *:not(.onetrust-vendors-list-handler),
#onetrust-consent-sdk .ot-dpd-desc *:not(.onetrust-vendors-list-handler),
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk #banner-options *,
#onetrust-banner-sdk .ot-cat-header {
color: #000000;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .banner-option-details {
background-color: #E9E9E9;}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk a[href],
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk a[href] font,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .ot-link-btn
{
color: #000000;
}#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-accept-btn-handler,
#onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-reject-all-handler {
background-color: #000000;border-color: #000000;
color: #FFFFFF;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk *:focus,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk:focus {
outline-color: #000000;
outline-width: 1px;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-pc-btn-handler,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-pc-btn-handler.cookie-setting-link {
color: #000000; border-color: #000000;
background-color:
#F2FAFD;
} /***********************************
* TCF BANNER
* APPLY TO EU TEMPLATE
***********************************/
/* Base Selectors - Close Button Container */
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-close-btn-container {
top: 25px;
right: 25px;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .onetrust-close-btn-ui {
width: 12px;
height: 12px;
}
/* Base Selectors - Button Group */
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-button-group {
text-align: center;
}
/* Base Selectors - Policy Text */
#onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-policy-text a.ot-sdk-show-settings {
margin-left: 0;
}
/* Base Selectors - Toggle Label */
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl .ot-label-status {
display: block!important;
}
/* HTML Overflow Handling */
html:has(>body>div#onetrust-consent-sdk.show-banner) {
overflow-y: hidden;
}
html:has(>body>div#onetrust-consent-sdk.show-banner>div.onetrust-pc-dark-filter[style*="none"]) {
overflow-y: auto;
}
/* Media Queries - Small Screens */
@media only screen and (min-width: 425px) and (max-width: 550px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk.ot-iab-2 #onetrust-button-group-parent #onetrust-button-group {
width: auto;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Medium Screens */
@media only screen and (min-width: 426px) and (max-width: 896px) {
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .onetrust-close-btn-ui {
top: 0;
right: 0;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Larger Screens */
@media (min-width: 896px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk .ot-sdk-three.ot-sdk-columns {
left: auto;
right: 4%;
margin-left: 0;
margin-top: 5%;
}
}
@media only screen and (min-width: 769px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-button-group {
margin-right: 0;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Desktop */
@media only screen and (min-width: 1024px){
#onetrust-banner-sdk.ot-iab-2 #onetrust-button-group-parent {
margin-top: .25em;
}
}
/* hides banner after it has already been interacted with */
#onetrust-consent-sdk:not(.show-banner) #onetrust-banner-sdk {

<div id="article-content_1-0" class="comp article-content mntl-block"><div id="mntl-sc-page_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-page mntl-block structured-content" data-sc-sticky-offset="90" data-sc-ad-label-height="24" data-sc-ad-track-spacing="100" data-sc-min-track-height="250" data-sc-max-track-height="600" data-sc-breakpoint="50em" data-sc-load-immediate="4" data-sc-content-positions="[1, 1250, 1550, 1950, 2350, 2750, 3150, 3550, 3950]" data-bind-scroll-on-start="true"><div id="mntl-sc-block_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block people-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-universal-callout theme-needtoknow" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"> <h3 class="mntl-sc-block-universal-callout__heading">NEED TO KNOW</h3>
<div class="mntl-sc-block-universal-callout__body"><ul><li>Tina Knowles reveals why the paperback edition of her memoir is slightly different from the hardcover </li><li><em>Matriarch: A Memoir</em> was initially released in hardcover on April 22, 2025</li><li>The mother of Beyoncé and Solange shares intimate details about her life, from parenthood to marriage and beyond</li></ul></div>
</div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_2-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> <a href="https://people.com/tag/tina-knowles/" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1">Tina Knowles</a> is giving fans even more in her memoir the second time around. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_3-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_4-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> During a conversation with moderator Angel Jennings at the 2026 L.A. Times Festival of Books on Saturday, April 18, the 72-year-old businesswoman and author opened up on why she decided to add a new chapter in the paperback version of <a href="https://tinaknowlesbook.com/" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="externalLink" data-ordinal="1" rel="nofollow noopener" target="_blank"><em>Matriarch</em></a>. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_5-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<figure id="mntl-sc-block_6-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block people-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image--no-theme no-theme figure-portrait figure-high-res"> <div class="figure-media">
<div class="img-placeholder" style="padding-bottom:155.6%;">
<img data-src="https://people.com/thmb/H8jFbqHfNz__gbY2gbJUD9dCaqE=/4000x0/filters:no_upscale():max_bytes(150000):strip_icc():focal(529x0:531x2):format(webp)/Matriarch-Oprahs-Book-Club-042326-9e1d52ca77e2485088ff06f1afc82dda.jpg" width="1060" height="1650" data-srcset="https://people.com/thmb/H8jFbqHfNz__gbY2gbJUD9dCaqE=/4000x0/filters:no_upscale():max_bytes(150000):strip_icc():focal(529x0:531x2):format(webp)/Matriarch-Oprahs-Book-Club-042326-9e1d52ca77e2485088ff06f1afc82dda.jpg 4000w" data-sizes="4000px" alt="Matriarch: Oprah's Book Club" class=" mntl-image mntl-image universal-image__image lazyloadwait" data-expand="300" id="mntl-sc-block-image_1-0" data-click-tracked="true" data-img-lightbox-variant="" data-tracking-container="true" data-img-lightbox="true">

<figcaption id="mntl-figure-caption_1-0" class="comp mntl-figure-caption text-utility-100 figure-article-caption"> <span class="figure-article-caption-text">Matriarch.</span>
<span class="figure-article-caption-owner"><p>One World</p></span>
</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_7-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “I end the book with finding out I have breast cancer and going through surgery, just a little bit about it,” Knowles said at the event, which was held at USC.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_8-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_9-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “And I've had so many people to ask me, ‘Well, what did you do? How did you take care of yourself? You know, how was your mental state?’ And all of that,” she explained. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_10-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_11-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block people-sc-block-spotlight--mid-circ mntl-sc-block-spotlight--mid-circ mntl-block"><h2 id="midcirc__heading_1-0" class="comp midcirc__heading mntl-text-block">Related Stories</h2>
<div id="midcirc__card-list_1-0" class="comp midcirc__card-list mntl-block"><a href="https://people.com/tina-knowles-almost-didnt-write-memoir-to-protect-kids-privacy-exclusive-11721595" rel="nocaes" data-card-index="0" data-click-tracked="false" id="midcirc-card_1-0" class="midcirc-card mntl-text-link " data-tracking-container="true"><div id="midcirc-card__title_1-0" class="comp midcirc-card__title mntl-text-block">Tina Knowles Almost Didn't Write Her Memoir to Protect Her Kids' Privacy (Exclusive)</div>

<div id="mntl-sc-block_12-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_13-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> The hardcover version of <a href="https://people.com/tina-knowles-new-memoir-matriarch-announcement-8735974" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1"><em>Matriarch: A Memoir</em></a> was published in April 2025 and followed her rise from a little girl in Texas to a “cultural icon, businesswoman, fashion designer and philanthropist,” according to an official synopsis.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_14-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_15-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> In the book, the mother of <a class="" href="https://people.com/tag/beyonce/" data-type="" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-ordinal="1" rel="noopener noreferrer">Beyoncé</a>, 44, and<a class="" href="https://people.com/tag/solange-knowles/" data-type="" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-ordinal="2" rel="noopener noreferrer"> Solange</a>, 39, revealed she was <a class="" href="https://people.com/tina-knowles-reveals-breast-cancer-battle-after-missed-mammogram-exclusive-11719247" data-type="" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-ordinal="3" rel="noopener noreferrer">diagnosed with breast cancer</a> after missing a mammogram. After its success, Knowles said she decided to share more of her journey.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_16-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_17-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block people-sc-block-featuredlink--people-app-promo mntl-block"><a href="https://people-app.onelink.me/HNIa/kz7l4cuf" target="_blank" rel="noopener nofollow nocaes" class="iosLink mntl-text-link " id="iosLink_1-0" data-tracking-container="true"><div id="people-app-promo__left_1-0" class="comp people-app-promo__left mntl-block"><svg class="icon people-app-promo__logo ">
<use xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" xlink:href="#people-app-promo__logo" href="#people-app-promo__logo"></use>
</svg></div>
<div id="people-app-promo__text_1-0" class="comp people-app-promo__text mntl-text-block"><b>Our new app is here!</b> Free, fun and full of exclusives. Scan to download now!</div>

width="168"
height="168"
class="img--noscript people-app-promo__img mntl-image universal-image__image"
/>
</noscript></a></div>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_18-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_19-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “I just thought that would be a good idea to update it and just put a paperback out because you reach a different audience with paperbacks,” Knowles said. “And I love the idea of that because I wanted that to happen, but I also wanted to catch people up on what happened since that time and all the lessons that I learned from having that experience because it was a tough ordeal.”
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_20-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_21-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Doctors discovered stage 1 breast cancer in her left breast, and Knowles underwent surgery to remove the tumor, as well as a breast reduction in 2024.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_22-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<figure id="mntl-sc-block_23-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block people-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image--no-theme no-theme figure-landscape figure-high-res"> <div class="figure-media">
<div class="img-placeholder" style="padding-bottom:66.6%;">
<img data-src="https://people.com/thmb/wo3fTIAM36dStOfvA--WxCaPetU=/4000x0/filters:no_upscale():max_bytes(150000):strip_icc():focal(999x0:1001x2):format(webp)/2026-Los-Angeles-Times-Festival-Of-Books-Tina-Knowles-042326-67c33aadf49449f2a504c673108221ed.jpg" width="2000" height="1333" data-srcset="https://people.com/thmb/wo3fTIAM36dStOfvA--WxCaPetU=/4000x0/filters:no_upscale():max_bytes(150000):strip_icc():focal(999x0:1001x2):format(webp)/2026-Los-Angeles-Times-Festival-Of-Books-Tina-Knowles-042326-67c33aadf49449f2a504c673108221ed.jpg 4000w" data-sizes="4000px" alt="Tina Knowles" class=" mntl-image mntl-image universal-image__image lazyloadwait" data-expand="300" id="mntl-sc-block-image_2-0" data-click-tracked="true" data-img-lightbox-variant="" data-tracking-container="true" data-img-lightbox="true">
<noscript>

</noscript>
</div> </div>
<figcaption id="mntl-figure-caption_2-0" class="comp mntl-figure-caption text-utility-100 figure-article-caption"> <span class="figure-article-caption-text">Tina Knowles (R) speaks at the 2026 Los Angeles Times Festival of Books at USC.</span>
<span class="figure-article-caption-owner"><p>Michael Tullberg/Getty</p></span>
</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_24-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Now Knowles is urging individuals to “get your mammograms.”
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_25-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_26-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “I could have missed mine because I missed two mammograms and didn't even realize it during COVID,” she said. “Finding out that I had breast cancer and catching it at the early stage was, you know, it's a miracle. So go and get your mammograms. [It] is so important to take care of yourself.”
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_27-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_28-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> The matriarch also shared her gratitude for her strong support system during her time of need.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_29-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_30-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “I don't care if they're related to you by blood or if they're friends, you need your inner circle during that time because no matter at what stage, when you hear the word ‘cancer,’ it is the most frightening thing that you can ever go through and you need people around you that are gonna support you and get you through it,” Knowles said. “And thank God I had plenty of people that helped me get through it, but some people are not that fortunate.”
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_31-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_32-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> <strong>Never miss a story — sign up for </strong><a href="https://people.com/people-news-daily-newsletter-sign-up-8692701" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1"><strong>PEOPLE's free daily newsletter</strong></a><strong> to stay up-to-date on the best of what PEOPLE has to offer, from celebrity news to compelling human interest stories.</strong>
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_33-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_34-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> <a href="https://bookshop.org/a/114093/9780593597422" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="externalLink" data-ordinal="1" rel="nofollow noopener" target="_blank"><em>Matriarch: A Memoir</em></a><em> </em>is available in paperback on April 28 and available for preorder now, wherever books are sold.
</p></div></div>

gold_text:
"You reach a different audience with paperbacks,” the mom to Beyoncé and Solange said
Tina Knowles is giving fans even more in her memoir the second time around. 

During a conversation with moderator Angel Jennings at the 2026 L.A. Times Festival of Books on Saturday, April 18, the 72-year-old businesswoman and author opened up on why she decided to add a new chapter in the paperback version of Matriarch. 
“I end the book with finding out I have breast cancer and going through surgery, just a little bit about it,” Knowles said at the event, which was held at USC.

“And I've had so many people to ask me, ‘Well, what did you do? How did you take care of yourself? You know, how was your mental state?’ And all of that,” she explained. 

The hardcover version of Matriarch: A Memoir was published in April 2025 and followed her rise from a little girl in Texas to a “cultural icon, businesswoman, fashion designer and philanthropist,” according to an official synopsis.

In the book, the mother of Beyoncé, 44, and Solange, 39, revealed she was diagnosed with breast cancer after missing a mammogram. After its success, Knowles said she decided to share more of her journey.

“I just thought that would be a good idea to update it and just put a paperback out because you reach a different audience with paperbacks,” Knowles said. “And I love the idea of that because I wanted that to happen, but I also wanted to catch people up on what happened since that time and all the lessons that I learned from having that experience because it was a tough ordeal.”

Doctors discovered stage 1 breast cancer in her left breast, and Knowles underwent surgery to remove the tumor, as well as a breast reduction in 2024.
Now Knowles is urging individuals to “get your mammograms.”

“I could have missed mine because I missed two mammograms and didn't even realize it during COVID,” she said. “Finding out that I had breast cancer and catching it at the early stage was, you know, it's a miracle. So go and get your mammograms. [It] is so important to take care of yourself.”

The matriarch also shared her gratitude for her strong support system during her time of need.

“I don't care if they're related to you by blood or if they're friends, you need your inner circle during that time because no matter at what stage, when you hear the word ‘cancer,’ it is the most frightening thing that you can ever go through and you need people around you that are gonna support you and get you through it,” Knowles said. “And thank God I had plenty of people that helped me get through it, but some people are not that fortunate.”
Matriarch: A Memoir  is available in paperback on April 28 and available for preorder now, wherever books are sold.

#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-policy-title,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-policy-text,
#onetrust-consent-sdk .ot-b-addl-desc,
#onetrust-consent-sdk .ot-dpd-desc,
#onetrust-consent-sdk .ot-dpd-title,

#onetrust-consent-sdk .ot-dpd-desc *:not(.onetrust-vendors-list-handler),
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk #banner-options *,
#onetrust-banner-sdk .ot-cat-header {
color: #000000;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .banner-option-details {
background-color: #E9E9E9;}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk a[href],
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk a[href] font,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .ot-link-btn
{
color: #000000;
}#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-accept-btn-handler,
#onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-reject-all-handler {
background-color: #000000;border-color: #000000;
color: #FFFFFF;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk *:focus,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk:focus {
outline-color: #000000;
outline-width: 1px;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-pc-btn-handler,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-pc-btn-handler.cookie-setting-link {
color: #000000; border-color: #000000;
background-color:

} /***********************************
* TCF BANNER
* APPLY TO EU TEMPLATE
***********************************/
/* Base Selectors - Close Button Container */
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-close-btn-container {
top: 25px;
right: 25px;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .onetrust-close-btn-ui {
width: 12px;
height: 12px;
}
/* Base Selectors - Button Group */
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-button-group {
text-align: center;
}
/* Base Selectors - Policy Text */
#onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-policy-text a.ot-sdk-show-settings {
margin-left: 0;
}
/* Base Selectors - Toggle Label */
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl .ot-label-status {
display: block!important;
}
/* HTML Overflow Handling */
html:has(>body>div#onetrust-consent-sdk.show-banner) {
overflow-y: hidden;
}
html:has(>body>div#onetrust-consent-sdk.show-banner>div.onetrust-pc-dark-filter[style*="none"]) {
overflow-y: auto;
}
/* Media Queries - Small Screens */
@media only screen and (min-width: 425px) and (max-width: 550px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk.ot-iab-2 #onetrust-button-group-parent #onetrust-button-group {
width: auto;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Medium Screens */
@media only screen and (min-width: 426px) and (max-width: 896px) {
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .onetrust-close-btn-ui {
top: 0;
right: 0;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Larger Screens */
@media (min-width: 896px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk .ot-sdk-three.ot-sdk-columns {
left: auto;
right: 4%;
margin-left: 0;
margin-top: 5%;
}
}
@media only screen and (min-width: 769px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-button-group {
margin-right: 0;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Desktop */
@media only screen and (min-width: 1024px){
#onetrust-banner-sdk.ot-iab-2 #onetrust-button-group-parent {
margin-top: .25em;
}
}
/* hides banner after it has already been interacted with */
#onetrust-consent-sdk:not(.show-banner) #onetrust-banner-sdk {
display: none;
}</style>

<div id="article-content_1-0" class="comp article-content mntl-block"><div id="mntl-sc-page_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-page mntl-block structured-content" data-sc-sticky-offset="90" data-sc-ad-label-height="24" data-sc-ad-track-spacing="100" data-sc-min-track-height="250" data-sc-max-track-height="600" data-sc-breakpoint="50em" data-sc-load-immediate="4" data-sc-content-positions="[1, 1250, 1550, 1950, 2350, 2750, 3150, 3550, 3950]" data-bind-scroll-on-start="true"><div id="mntl-sc-block_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block people-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-universal-callout theme-needtoknow" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"> <h3 class="mntl-sc-block-universal-callout__heading">NEED TO KNOW</h3>
<div class="mntl-sc-block-universal-callout__body"><ul><li>A 51-year-old passenger died on a Cathay Pacific flight from Manchester, U.K., to Hong Kong despite the first aid efforts of the crew and a doctor on board</li><li>The airline confirmed the passenger "became unwell during the flight" before landing at Hong Kong International Airport on April 24</li><li>Authorities are conducting an autopsy to determine the cause of death</li></ul></div>
</div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_2-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> A 51-year-old plane passenger died during an international flight to Hong Kong on Friday, the airline has confirmed. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_3-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_4-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Cathay Pacific flight CX216 departed Manchester Airport in England on Thursday, April 23, according to <a class="" href="https://www.flightaware.com/live/flight/CPA216/history/20260423/1020Z/EGCC/VHHH" data-type="" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-ordinal="1" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank">FlightAware</a>. The Boeing 777-300ER landed at the Hong Kong International Airport the following morning just before 7 a.m. local time. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_5-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_6-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> However, around 6:15 a.m. on Friday morning, local police say they received a report that a passenger had lost consciousness on an inbound flight, according to the <a class="" href="https://hongkongfp.com/2026/04/24/passenger-dies-after-collapsing-on-cathay-pacific-flight-from-uk/" data-type="" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-ordinal="1" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank"><em>Hong Kong Free Press</em></a>. The crew on board alerted Hong Kong’s air traffic control, who notified emergency services.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_7-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_8-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> A spokesperson for Hong Kong International airport tells PEOPLE the Airport Authority Hong Kong “contacted the Port Health Division of the Department of Health immediately to prepare for providing medical assistance after the flight landed at Hong Kong International Airport.”
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_9-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"><div id="mm-ads-native-fluid_1-0" class="comp mm-ads-native-fluid mm-ads-native " style="--native-ad-height: auto" data-right-rail-index="2"><div id="mm-ads-native__adunit_1-0" class="comp mm-ads-native__adunit mm-ads-gpt-dynamic-adunit mm-ads-gpt-adunit scads-to-load gpt native js-immediate-ad js-immediate-ad-loaded">

</div></div></div>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_13-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> In a statement shared with PEOPLE from a Cathay Pacific spokesperson, the company confirmed a passenger “became unwell during the flight.”
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_14-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_15-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “The crew and a doctor on board immediately provided first aid; however the passenger had unfortunately passed away. An ambulance was called, and paramedics were on board immediately after the aircraft landed at Hong Kong International Airport,” they added. “We are deeply saddened by this and extend our condolences to the passenger’s family.”
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_16-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_17-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> The man was an insurance broker who immigrated to the U.K. with his family, <a class="" href="https://www.hk01.com/%E7%AA%81%E7%99%BC/60343375/%E5%9C%8B%E6%B3%B0%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E8%8B%B1%E5%9C%8B%E6%9B%BC%E5%9F%8E%E8%BF%94%E6%B8%AF%E5%AE%A2%E6%A9%9F-51%E6%AD%B2%E7%94%B7%E4%BF%9D%E9%9A%AA%E7%B6%93%E7%B4%80%E6%9A%88%E5%80%92-%E9%99%8D%E8%90%BD%E5%BE%8C%E8%AD%89%E5%AF%A6%E4%B8%8D%E6%B2%BB#google_vignette" data-type="" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-ordinal="1" rel="noopener noreferrer nofollow" target="_blank">HK01</a> reports. He was allegedly diagnosed with cancer last year and frequently traveled between Hong Kong and the U.K. for medical checkups and business. He was reportedly traveling with his wife at the time. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_18-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_19-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Authorities say an autopsy is already underway, according to the <em>Hong Kong Free Press</em>. A cause of death has yet to be determined. PEOPLE has reached out to Hong Kong Police for comment.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_20-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_21-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> The flight was in the air for a total travel time of 12 hours and 48 minutes, according to FlightAware. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_22-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>

<div id="mntl-sc-block_24-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_25-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Earlier this month, <a class="" href="https://people.com/woman-suddenly-dies-on-long-haul-flight-from-new-york-to-new-zealand-11949234" data-type="" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-ordinal="1" rel="noopener noreferrer">a woman died on a long-haul flight from New York to Auckland, New Zealand</a>. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_26-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_27-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> The death occurred on Qantas flight QF4 on Monday, April 14, according to the <a class="" href="https://www.nzherald.co.nz/nz/police-investigate-womans-death-on-qantas-flight-from-new-york-to-auckland/5RLLB4SPDNC5JHTRDN4MP7RZAU/" data-type="" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-ordinal="1" rel="noopener noreferrer" target="_blank"><em>New Zealand Herald</em></a><em>.</em> Emergency services rushed to the aircraft upon arrival, per the outlet. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_28-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_29-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> In a statement shared with PEOPLE on April 14, New Zealand Police confirmed that they launched an investigation into the circumstances of the woman’s death. 
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_30-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_31-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> <strong>Never miss a story — sign up for </strong><a class="" href="https://people.com/people-news-daily-newsletter-sign-up-8692701" data-type="" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-ordinal="1" rel="noopener noreferrer"><strong>PEOPLE's free daily newsletter</strong></a><strong> to stay up-to-date on the best of what PEOPLE has to offer, from celebrity news to compelling human interest stories.</strong>
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_32-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_33-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> A Qantas spokesperson told PEOPLE that attempts had been made to provide the woman with emergency care before the aircraft arrived.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_34-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_35-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Details of the woman’s identity were not shared publicly. 
</p></div></div>

function overwriteIframeTitle(mutationList, embedTitle, observer) {
for (const mutation of mutationList) {
if (mutation.type === 'childList') {
const parentNode = mutationList[0].target;
// Grab iframe from parentNode
const iframe = parentNode.querySelector('iframe');
if (iframe !== null) {
// Overwrite iframe title attr with title from Selene
iframe.title = embedTitle;
observer.disconnect();
// Early return, prevent further looping for other mutations
return;
}
}
}

}

const options = { rootMargin: '300px' };

observer.observe(embed);
observeChildNodeChanges(embed);
});
}
}

The 51-year-old passenger reportedly collapsed just before landing in Hong Kong on Friday, April 24
A 51-year-old plane passenger died during an international flight to Hong Kong on Friday, the airline has confirmed. 

Cathay Pacific flight CX216 departed Manchester Airport in England on Thursday, April 23, according to FlightAware. The Boeing 777-300ER landed at the Hong Kong International Airport the following morning just before 7 a.m. local time. 

However, around 6:15 a.m. on Friday morning, local police say they received a report that a passenger had lost consciousness on an inbound flight, according to the Hong Kong Free Press. The crew on board alerted Hong Kong’s air traffic control, who notified emergency services.

A spokesperson for Hong Kong International airport tells PEOPLE the Airport Authority Hong Kong “contacted the Port Health Division of the Department of Health immediately to prepare for providing medical assistance after the flight landed at Hong Kong International Airport.”
n a statement shared with PEOPLE from a Cathay Pacific spokesperson, the company confirmed a passenger “became unwell during the flight.”

“The crew and a doctor on board immediately provided first aid; however the passenger had unfortunately passed away. An ambulance was called, and paramedics were on board immediately after the aircraft landed at Hong Kong International Airport,” they added. “We are deeply saddened by this and extend our condolences to the passenger’s family.”

The man was an insurance broker who immigrated to the U.K. with his family, HK01 reports. He was allegedly diagnosed with cancer last year and frequently traveled between Hong Kong and the U.K. for medical checkups and business. He was reportedly traveling with his wife at the time. 

Authorities say an autopsy is already underway, according to the Hong Kong Free Press. A cause of death has yet to be determined. PEOPLE has reached out to Hong Kong Police for comment.

The flight was in the air for a total travel time of 12 hours and 48 minutes, according to FlightAware. 
Earlier this month, a woman died on a long-haul flight from New York to Auckland, New Zealand. 

The death occurred on Qantas flight QF4 on Monday, April 14, according to the New Zealand Herald. Emergency services rushed to the aircraft upon arrival, per the outlet. 

In a statement shared with PEOPLE on April 14, New Zealand Police confirmed that they launched an investigation into the circumstances of the woman’s death. 
A Qantas spokesperson told PEOPLE that attempts had been made to provide the woman with emergency care before the aircraft arrived.

Details of the woman’s identity were not shared publicly.

background-color: #E9E9E9;}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk a[href],

} /***********************************
* TCF BANNER
* APPLY TO EU TEMPLATE
***********************************/
/* Base Selectors - Close Button Container */
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-close-btn-container {
top: 25px;
right: 25px;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .onetrust-close-btn-ui {
width: 12px;
height: 12px;
}
/* Base Selectors - Button Group */
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-button-group {
text-align: center;
}
/* Base Selectors - Policy Text */
#onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-policy-text a.ot-sdk-show-settings {
margin-left: 0;
}
/* Base Selectors - Toggle Label */
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl .ot-label-status {
display: block!important;
}
/* HTML Overflow Handling */
html:has(>body>div#onetrust-consent-sdk.show-banner) {
overflow-y: hidden;
}
html:has(>body>div#onetrust-consent-sdk.show-banner>div.onetrust-pc-dark-filter[style*="none"]) {
overflow-y: auto;
}
/* Media Queries - Small Screens */
@media only screen and (min-width: 425px) and (max-width: 550px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk.ot-iab-2 #onetrust-button-group-parent #onetrust-button-group {
width: auto;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Medium Screens */
@media only screen and (min-width: 426px) and (max-width: 896px) {
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .onetrust-close-btn-ui {
top: 0;
right: 0;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Larger Screens */
@media (min-width: 896px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk .ot-sdk-three.ot-sdk-columns {
left: auto;
right: 4%;
margin-left: 0;
margin-top: 5%;
}
}
@media only screen and (min-width: 769px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-button-group {
margin-right: 0;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Desktop */
@media only screen and (min-width: 1024px){
#onetrust-banner-sdk.ot-iab-2 #onetrust-button-group-parent {
margin-top: .25em;
}
}
/* hides banner after it has already been interacted with */
#onetrust-consent-sdk:not(.show-banner) #onetrust-banner-sdk {
display: none;
}</style>

<h1 class="article-heading text-headline-400">Andy Weir, Robinne Lee and More Reveal What Makes a Successful Book-to-Screen Adaptation</h1>
<p class="article-subheading text-utility-300">"I feel like if something sticks to the spirit of the source material, that's successful,” said 'The Hunting Wives' author May Cobb</p>

<figcaption id="primary-image__figcap_1-0" class="comp primary-image__figcap mntl-figure-caption figure-article-caption"> <span class="figure-article-caption-text">A photo of Andy Weir and Robinne Lee.</span>
<span class="figure-article-caption-owner">Credit : <p>Jason Mendez/Getty; Arnold Turner/Getty</p></span>
</figcaption></figure>
<div id="article-content_1-0" class="comp article-content mntl-block"><div id="mntl-sc-page_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-page mntl-block structured-content" data-sc-sticky-offset="90" data-sc-ad-label-height="24" data-sc-ad-track-spacing="100" data-sc-min-track-height="250" data-sc-max-track-height="600" data-sc-breakpoint="50em" data-sc-load-immediate="4" data-sc-content-positions="[1, 1250, 1550, 1950, 2350, 2750, 3150, 3550, 3950]" data-bind-scroll-on-start="true"><div id="mntl-sc-block_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block people-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-universal-callout theme-needtoknow" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"> <h3 class="mntl-sc-block-universal-callout__heading">NEED TO KNOW</h3>
<div class="mntl-sc-block-universal-callout__body"><ul><li>Several bestselling authors came together to discuss what makes a successful screen adaptation during a panel at BookCon in New York City on April 19</li><li>Robinne Lee, author of <em>The Idea of You, </em>noted that adaptations needed to strike a “balance” between attracting a “wider” audience but also keeping true to the book</li><li>Andy Weir, author of <em>Project Hail Mary, </em>said that writers can be biased when it comes to on-screen adaptations</li></ul></div>
</div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_2-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Translating a book to screen isn’t easy — and authors know this better than anyone.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_3-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_4-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Bestselling authors <a href="https://people.com/andy-weir-new-book-exclusive-11934437" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1">Andy Weir</a>, <a href="https://people.com/robinne-lee-crash-into-me-cover-reveal-exclusive-11884583" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="2">Robinne Lee</a>, <a href="https://people.com/may-cobb-the-hunting-wives-profile-exclusive-11783569" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="3">May Cobb</a> and Emily St. John Mandel — each of whom have had books adapted for the screen — weighed in during a panel at BookCon on April 19 on what they think makes a successful screen adaptation.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_5-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_6-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “Personally for my work or just in general, I feel like we all know that there's going to be a lot of changes because there has to be to adapt to a new medium. So I feel like if something sticks to the spirit of the source material, that's successful,” said <em>The Hunting Wives</em> author Cobb.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_7-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_8-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Lee — who wrote <em>The Idea of You</em>, which was adapted into an Amazon Prime film starring <a class="" href="https://people.com/tag/anne-hathaway/" data-type="" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-ordinal="1" rel="noopener noreferrer">Anne Hathaway</a> — agreed with Cobb, adding that adaptations needed to strike a “balance” between attracting a “wider” audience but also keeping true to the book.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_9-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"><div id="mm-ads-native-fluid_1-0" class="comp mm-ads-native-fluid mm-ads-native " style="--native-ad-height: auto" data-right-rail-index="2"><div id="mm-ads-native__adunit_1-0" class="comp mm-ads-native__adunit mm-ads-gpt-dynamic-adunit mm-ads-gpt-adunit scads-to-load gpt native js-immediate-ad js-immediate-ad-loaded">
<div id="native" class="wrapper" data-type="native" data-pos="native" data-priority="4" data-sizes="[[1, 3],"fluid",[600, 250]]" data-rtb="true" data-wait-for-third-party="false" data-targeting="{}"></div>
</div></div></div>

<div class="img-placeholder" style="padding-bottom:168.7%;">

<figcaption id="mntl-figure-caption_1-0" class="comp mntl-figure-caption text-utility-100 figure-article-caption"> <span class="figure-article-caption-text">Robinne Lee attends 13th Annual Women In Film in 2020.</span>
<span class="figure-article-caption-owner"><p>Rodin Eckenroth/FilmMagic</p></span>
</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_11-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “I think it's important to keep the spirit of the book and to ideally, when you are creating something for the screen, whether it's a large or small screen, you're manufacturing something for a larger and wider audience,” she said. “But ideally you're not going to alienate those who fell in love with the book.”
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_12-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_15-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “So it's like a balancing act of how can I be true to what readers fell in love with and still create something that's going to appeal to a wide audience?" she continued.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_16-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_17-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> It can be eye-opening for authors to see the new version of their work, according to Mandel, whose novel <em>Station Eleven </em>was adapted into an HBO Max limited series. She shared that she thought it was “surreal” seeing her book on the screen for the first time, especially since she “was not deeply involved in the adaptation.”
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_18-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_19-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “It was only about two months before everybody else saw it. And I was like, ‘Wow, they changed every single plot point, every single one,’ ” she recalled. “But they did retain the spirit of the original, so it felt to me like a successful adaptation.”
</p>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_23-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> As for Weir — who wrote <em>Project Hail Mary</em>, which was recently adapted into a film starring <a class="" href="https://people.com/tag/ryan-gosling/" data-type="" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-ordinal="1" rel="noopener noreferrer">Ryan Gosling </a>— he noted that it can be tough for authors to give their opinion on what a successful adaptation would look like, given how biased they are as the creator.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_24-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_25-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> “You're asking a bunch of writers what we think the most important aspect of page-to-screen adaptation is so we're going to skew heavily toward staying true to the source material because we <em>made</em> the source material,” Weir said.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_26-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>

function loadDigioh() {
if (!Mntl.digioh.initialized) {
Mntl.digioh.types.load();
}

window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('digiohLoaded'));

function resetTimer() {
clearTimeout(inactivityTimer);
inactivityTimer = setTimeout(loadDigioh, Mntl.digioh.timerDelay);
};
// Debounce high frequency events
const throttleReset = Mntl.throttle(resetTimer, 250);
function removeEventListeners() {
window.removeEventListener('mousemove', throttleReset);
window.removeEventListener('scroll', throttleReset);
window.removeEventListener('click', resetTimer);
window.removeEventListener('keydown', resetTimer);
window.removeEventListener('touchstart', resetTimer);

}

window.addEventListener('click', resetTimer);
window.addEventListener('keydown', resetTimer);
window.addEventListener('touchstart', resetTimer);
window.addEventListener('digiohLoaded', removeEventListeners);
}, { once: true });

embed() {
function overwriteIframeTitle(mutationList, embedTitle, observer) {
for (const mutation of mutationList) {
if (mutation.type === 'childList') {
const parentNode = mutationList[0].target;
// Grab iframe from parentNode
const iframe = parentNode.querySelector('iframe');
if (iframe !== null) {
// Overwrite iframe title attr with title from Selene
iframe.title = embedTitle;
observer.disconnect();
// Early return, prevent further looping for other mutations
return;
}
}
}
}
function observeChildNodeChanges(embed) {
const config = {
childList: true,
subtree: true
};
/* Explicitly use arrow function for the callback executed on
observed node to pass in embed title to overwrite iframe title */
const observer = new MutationObserver((mutationList) => {
overwriteIframeTitle(mutationList, embed.dataset.title, observer);
});
observer.observe(embed, config);
}
function observerCallback(entries, observer) {
entries.forEach((entry) => {
if (entry.isIntersecting) {
const digiohEmbed = entry.target;
if (Mntl.digioh.initialized) {
observer.unobserve(digiohEmbed);
} else {
Mntl.digioh.types.load(() => observer.unobserve(digiohEmbed));
}
}
});
}
const digiohEmbeds = document.querySelectorAll('.mntl-sc-block-digiohembed__container');
const options = { rootMargin: '300px' };
const observer = new IntersectionObserver(observerCallback, options);
digiohEmbeds.forEach((embed) => {
observer.observe(embed);
observeChildNodeChanges(embed);
});
}
}
};
if (Mntl.digioh.type) Mntl.digioh.types[Mntl.digioh.type]();
})(window.Mntl || {});

"I feel like if something sticks to the spirit of the source material, that's successful,” said 'The Hunting Wives' author May Cobb
Translating a book to screen isn’t easy — and authors know this better than anyone.

Bestselling authors Andy Weir, Robinne Lee, May Cobb and Emily St. John Mandel — each of whom have had books adapted for the screen — weighed in during a panel at BookCon on April 19 on what they think makes a successful screen adaptation.

“Personally for my work or just in general, I feel like we all know that there's going to be a lot of changes because there has to be to adapt to a new medium. So I feel like if something sticks to the spirit of the source material, that's successful,” said The Hunting Wives author Cobb.

Lee — who wrote The Idea of You, which was adapted into an Amazon Prime film starring Anne Hathaway — agreed with Cobb, adding that adaptations needed to strike a “balance” between attracting a “wider” audience but also keeping true to the book.
“I think it's important to keep the spirit of the book and to ideally, when you are creating something for the screen, whether it's a large or small screen, you're manufacturing something for a larger and wider audience,” she said. “But ideally you're not going to alienate those who fell in love with the book.”
“So it's like a balancing act of how can I be true to what readers fell in love with and still create something that's going to appeal to a wide audience?" she continued.

It can be eye-opening for authors to see the new version of their work, according to Mandel, whose novel Station Eleven was adapted into an HBO Max limited series. She shared that she thought it was “surreal” seeing her book on the screen for the first time, especially since she “was not deeply involved in the adaptation.”

“It was only about two months before everybody else saw it. And I was like, ‘Wow, they changed every single plot point, every single one,’ ” she recalled. “But they did retain the spirit of the original, so it felt to me like a successful adaptation.”
As for Weir — who wrote Project Hail Mary, which was recently adapted into a film starring Ryan Gosling — he noted that it can be tough for authors to give their opinion on what a successful adaptation would look like, given how biased they are as the creator.

“You're asking a bunch of writers what we think the most important aspect of page-to-screen adaptation is so we're going to skew heavily toward staying true to the source material because we made the source material,” Weir said.

“So we tend to define the adaptation in terms of, ‘How closely did you stay to my story and how much did you mess it up?’" he added. "So we have our bias on this.”
But by all objective metrics, they're doing just fine. Project Hail Mary has grossed over $573 million in the box office as of April 20. The Hunting Wives also became the top-streamed series in the U.S. shortly after its August 2025 release, according to The Hollywood Reporter.

#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-policy-text,

#onetrust-consent-sdk .ot-dpd-desc *:not(.onetrust-vendors-list-handler),
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk #banner-options *,
#onetrust-banner-sdk .ot-cat-header {
color: #000000;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .banner-option-details {
background-color: #E9E9E9;}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk a[href],
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk a[href] font,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .ot-link-btn
{
color: #000000;
}#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-accept-btn-handler,
#onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-reject-all-handler {
background-color: #000000;border-color: #000000;
color: #FFFFFF;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk *:focus,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk:focus {
outline-color: #000000;
outline-width: 1px;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-pc-btn-handler,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-pc-btn-handler.cookie-setting-link {
color: #000000; border-color: #000000;
background-color:

} /***********************************
* TCF BANNER
* APPLY TO EU TEMPLATE
***********************************/
/* Base Selectors - Close Button Container */
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-close-btn-container {
top: 25px;
right: 25px;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .onetrust-close-btn-ui {
width: 12px;
height: 12px;
}
/* Base Selectors - Button Group */
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-button-group {
text-align: center;
}
/* Base Selectors - Policy Text */
#onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-policy-text a.ot-sdk-show-settings {
margin-left: 0;
}
/* Base Selectors - Toggle Label */
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl .ot-label-status {
display: block!important;
}
/* HTML Overflow Handling */
html:has(>body>div#onetrust-consent-sdk.show-banner) {
overflow-y: hidden;
}
html:has(>body>div#onetrust-consent-sdk.show-banner>div.onetrust-pc-dark-filter[style*="none"]) {
overflow-y: auto;
}
/* Media Queries - Small Screens */
@media only screen and (min-width: 425px) and (max-width: 550px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk.ot-iab-2 #onetrust-button-group-parent #onetrust-button-group {
width: auto;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Medium Screens */
@media only screen and (min-width: 426px) and (max-width: 896px) {
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .onetrust-close-btn-ui {
top: 0;
right: 0;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Larger Screens */
@media (min-width: 896px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk .ot-sdk-three.ot-sdk-columns {
left: auto;
right: 4%;
margin-left: 0;
margin-top: 5%;
}
}
@media only screen and (min-width: 769px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-button-group {
margin-right: 0;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Desktop */
@media only screen and (min-width: 1024px){
#onetrust-banner-sdk.ot-iab-2 #onetrust-button-group-parent {
margin-top: .25em;
}
}
/* hides banner after it has already been interacted with */
#onetrust-consent-sdk:not(.show-banner) #onetrust-banner-sdk {
display: none;
}</style>

<h1 class="article-heading text-headline-400">Breast Cancer Awareness: Empowering Stories and All the Facts</h1>
<p class="article-subheading text-utility-300">PEOPLE and Verywell Health teamed up to bring you important information about breast cancer and powerful interviews with women with the condition</p>
<div id="mntl-article-meta-dynamic_1-0" class="comp mntl-article-meta-dynamic mntl-article-meta mntl-block pre-content" data-tracking-container="true"><div id="bylines_1-0" class="comp bylines people-bylines mntl-bylines text-utility-200"><div id="mntl-bylines__group_1-0" class="comp mntl-bylines__group mntl-block mntl-bylines__group--author"><div id="mntl-bylines__item_1-0" class="comp mntl-bylines__item mntl-attribution__item mntl-attribution__item--has-date">

<div class="mntl-author-tooltip__bottom">

</div>
</div></div>

<div class="mntl-attribution__item-date">Updated on April 7, 2026 11:21AM EDT</div></div>
</div></div></div>
</div><div class="loc article-content">
<div id="article-content_1-0" class="comp article-content mntl-block"><div id="mntl-sc-page_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-page mntl-block" data-sc-sticky-offset="60" data-sc-ad-label-height="24" data-sc-ad-track-spacing="100" data-sc-min-track-height="250" data-sc-max-track-height="600" data-sc-breakpoint="50em" data-sc-load-immediate="0" data-sc-content-positions="[1, 1000]" data-bind-scroll-on-start="true"><p id="mntl-sc-block_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Despite many advances in breast cancer treatment in the past few decades, which have prolonged life expectancy and improved the quality of life of those with the condition, the disease remains incredibly common, with rising rates of diagnosis among young women.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_2-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_3-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> However, as PEOPLE Editor in Chief Charlotte Triggs points out in her conversation with Verywell Health's Chief Medical Officer, Dr. Sohaib Imtiaz, there are also reasons to be hopeful — and more than ever, people are sharing their stories in hopes of raising awareness, funds for research and making others feel less alone.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_4-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_5-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> From what to know about a breast cancer diagnosis (and what to expect from the treatment), to powerful interviews with real people who have faced a metastatic prognosis including CNN's Sara Sidner, PEOPLE and Verywell Health's People Like Us condition center is designed to equip you with information, insight and hope.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_6-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_7-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block people-sc-block-spotlight mntl-sc-block-universal-spotlight mntl-block theme-single-card"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-universal-spotlight__heading_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-universal-spotlight__heading mntl-block"><span id="mntl-sc-block-universal-spotlight__heading-text_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-universal-spotlight__heading-text mntl-text-block">Letter from the Editor</span></h3>
<div id="mntl-sc-block-universal-spotlight__articles_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-universal-spotlight__articles mntl-document-spotlight mntl-document-card-list mntl-card-list mntl-block" data-chunk="36"><a id="mntl-card-list-items_1-0" class="comp mntl-card-list-items mntl-document-card mntl-card card card--no-image" data-doc-id="11689213" data-tax-levels="" href="https://people.com/people-like-us-breast-cancer-editors-letter-11689213" data-ordinal="1">

<div class="card__content " data-tag="Health">
<div class="card__header"></div>
<span class="card__title"><span class="card__title-text ">A Letter from the Editor: What to Know About Breast Cancer Now</span></span>
<div class="card__byline mntl-card__byline " data-byline="By Charlotte Triggs"></div>
<div id="card__description_1-0" class="comp card__description mntl-block text-utility-200"></div> </div>
</a></div></div>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_8-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_9-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> CNN anchor Sara Sidner underwent treatment for her stage 3 breast cancer while going to work, and while she struggled with the implications of losing her hair and not feeling her best on-camera, she now ha
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_10-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_11-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block people-sc-block-spotlight mntl-sc-block-universal-spotlight mntl-block theme-single-card"><h3 id="mntl-sc-block-universal-spotlight__heading_2-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-universal-spotlight__heading mntl-block"><span id="mntl-sc-block-universal-spotlight__heading-text_2-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-universal-spotlight__heading-text mntl-text-block">Finding Joy </span></h3>
<div id="mntl-sc-block-universal-spotlight__articles_2-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block-universal-spotlight__articles mntl-document-spotlight mntl-document-card-list mntl-card-list mntl-block" data-chunk="36"><a id="mntl-card-list-items_2-0" class="comp mntl-card-list-items mntl-document-card mntl-card card card--no-image" data-doc-id="11943623" data-tax-levels="" href="https://people.com/cnn-anchor-sara-sidner-breast-cancer-exclusive-11943623" data-ordinal="1">

.then(() => {
loginViafoura();
})
.catch(() => {
logoutViafoura();
});
}
}, CHECK_REFRESH_INTERVAL);

if (utils.isLoggedIn()) {

interval = initRefreshInterval();
})
.catch(() => {
logoutViafoura();
});
} else {
logoutViafoura();
}
}

onAuthenticate(method);
return false;
}
return {
channel,
event,
args
};

if (utils.isLoggedIn()) {

if (method === "login"){

}
});
} else if (method === "login"){

} else {

}
}

}

function logoutViafoura() {

window.vf.session.logout();

} catch (error) {

}
}

gold_text:
PEOPLE and Verywell Health teamed up to bring you important information about breast cancer and powerful interviews with women with the condition

Despite many advances in breast cancer treatment in the past few decades, which have prolonged life expectancy and improved the quality of life of those with the condition, the disease remains incredibly common, with rising rates of diagnosis among young women.
However, as PEOPLE Editor in Chief Charlotte Triggs points out in her conversation with Verywell Health's Chief Medical Officer, Dr. Sohaib Imtiaz, there are also reasons to be hopeful — and more than ever, people are sharing their stories in hopes of raising awareness, funds for research and making others feel less alone.

From what to know about a breast cancer diagnosis (and what to expect from the treatment), to powerful interviews with real people who have faced a metastatic prognosis including CNN's Sara Sidner, PEOPLE and Verywell Health's People Like Us condition center is designed to equip you with information, insight and hope.
CNN anchor Sara Sidner underwent treatment for her stage 3 breast cancer while going to work, and while she struggled with the implications of losing her hair and not feeling her best on-camera, she now ha
What to know about breast cancer right now, from our partners at Verywell Health.

The Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders use their platform to bring awareness to early detection and screening.
Read inspiring, moving stories from women across the country who are undergoing treatment for breast cancer, while doing the hard work of parenting, studying and helping advocate for others in the same situation.
Another mom undergoing breast cancer shares her real-life insight as to what's been helpful to her and her family, for those looking to support a loved one through their treatment.
Sara Sidner reflects on how facing some of her fears about her on-camera appearance made her come out on the other side confident and appreciative of what she has.
Learn more about the side effects that aren't just physical once you're facing a cancer diagnosis.

#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-policy-text,

#onetrust-consent-sdk .ot-dpd-desc *:not(.onetrust-vendors-list-handler),
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk #banner-options *,
#onetrust-banner-sdk .ot-cat-header {
color: #000000;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .banner-option-details {
background-color: #E9E9E9;}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk a[href],
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk a[href] font,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .ot-link-btn
{
color: #000000;
}#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-accept-btn-handler,
#onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-reject-all-handler {
background-color: #000000;border-color: #000000;
color: #FFFFFF;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk *:focus,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk:focus {
outline-color: #000000;
outline-width: 1px;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-pc-btn-handler,
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-pc-btn-handler.cookie-setting-link {
color: #000000; border-color: #000000;
background-color:

} /***********************************
* TCF BANNER
* APPLY TO EU TEMPLATE
***********************************/
/* Base Selectors - Close Button Container */
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-close-btn-container {
top: 25px;
right: 25px;
}
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .onetrust-close-btn-ui {
width: 12px;
height: 12px;
}
/* Base Selectors - Button Group */
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-button-group {
text-align: center;
}
/* Base Selectors - Policy Text */
#onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-policy-text a.ot-sdk-show-settings {
margin-left: 0;
}
/* Base Selectors - Toggle Label */
#onetrust-pc-sdk .ot-tgl .ot-label-status {
display: block!important;
}
/* HTML Overflow Handling */
html:has(>body>div#onetrust-consent-sdk.show-banner) {
overflow-y: hidden;
}
html:has(>body>div#onetrust-consent-sdk.show-banner>div.onetrust-pc-dark-filter[style*="none"]) {
overflow-y: auto;
}
/* Media Queries - Small Screens */
@media only screen and (min-width: 425px) and (max-width: 550px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk.ot-iab-2 #onetrust-button-group-parent #onetrust-button-group {
width: auto;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Medium Screens */
@media only screen and (min-width: 426px) and (max-width: 896px) {
#onetrust-consent-sdk #onetrust-banner-sdk .onetrust-close-btn-ui {
top: 0;
right: 0;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Larger Screens */
@media (min-width: 896px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk .ot-sdk-three.ot-sdk-columns {
left: auto;
right: 4%;
margin-left: 0;
margin-top: 5%;
}
}
@media only screen and (min-width: 769px) {
#onetrust-banner-sdk #onetrust-button-group {
margin-right: 0;
}
}
/* Media Queries - Desktop */
@media only screen and (min-width: 1024px){
#onetrust-banner-sdk.ot-iab-2 #onetrust-button-group-parent {
margin-top: .25em;
}
}
/* hides banner after it has already been interacted with */
#onetrust-consent-sdk:not(.show-banner) #onetrust-banner-sdk {
display: none;
}</style>

<div id="article-content_1-0" class="comp article-content mntl-block"><div id="mntl-sc-page_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-page mntl-block structured-content" data-sc-sticky-offset="90" data-sc-ad-label-height="24" data-sc-ad-track-spacing="100" data-sc-min-track-height="250" data-sc-max-track-height="600" data-sc-breakpoint="50em" data-sc-load-immediate="4" data-sc-content-positions="[1, 1250, 1550, 1950, 2350, 2750, 3150, 3550, 3950]" data-bind-scroll-on-start="true"><div id="mntl-sc-block_1-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block people-sc-block-callout mntl-sc-block-universal-callout theme-needtoknow" data-tracking-id="mntl-sc-block-callout" data-tracking-container="true"> <h3 class="mntl-sc-block-universal-callout__heading">NEED TO KNOW</h3>
<div class="mntl-sc-block-universal-callout__body"><ul><li>Anne Hathaway is on the cover of PEOPLE's World's Most Beautiful issue</li><li>She says being a mom to two boys, Jonathan, 10, and Jack, 6, has influenced her fashion choices and keeps her ready for anything</li><li>Hathaway jokes about the differences between raising boys and girls and calls motherhood a "beautiful ongoing poem"</li></ul></div>
</div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_2-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> <a href="https://people.com/tag/anne-hathaway/" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1">Anne Hathaway</a> has to be ready to play basketball at a moment's notice.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_3-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_4-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> The actress — who shares two sons Jonathan, 10, and Jack, 6, with <a href="https://people.com/anne-hathaway-dream-husband-adam-shulman-worlds-most-beautiful-11950636" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1">husband Adam Shulman</a> — tells PEOPLE in her <a href="https://people.com/anne-hathaway-worlds-most-beautiful-cover-2026-11950632" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="2">cover story for World's Most Beautiful 2026</a> about her favorite parts about being a "boy mom."
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_5-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_6-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> "I’m a tomboy! I love that being a boy mom has really informed my fashion," she says in the issue on newsstands Friday. "I have to always be able to go from a serious business meeting to, like, a basketball court. You have to be able to play pickup basketball at any point, in any outfit. So [I tell myself]: When you get dressed in the morning, dress wisely because you 100 percent are going to have a projectile thrown at you probably by the end of breakfast."
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_7-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<figure id="mntl-sc-block_8-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block people-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image--no-theme no-theme figure-landscape figure-high-res"> <div class="figure-media">

<span class="figure-article-caption-owner"><p><a href="https://www.instagram.com/jonnymarlow" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="externalLink" data-ordinal="1" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Jonny Marlow</a></p></span>
</figcaption></figure>
<p id="mntl-sc-block_9-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> The <em>Devil Wears Prada 2</em> star laughed with <a href="https://people.com/from-the-editor-newsletter-sign-up-form-11953046" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1">PEOPLE GM and Editor in Chief Charlotte Triggs</a> about what it's like to be a boy mom like herself vs. a girl mom like Triggs (who has three daughters).
</p>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_13-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> "Did you ever see the video about boy moms vs. girl moms? Oh my gosh. It's so funny," says Hathaway, 43. "It's like girl moms sit and they're like, coloring with their daughter and they're like, 'What a beautiful piece of art?' And it's like, so quiet. Boy moms, they're like, 'Where's your weapons' basket?'" she jokes.
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_14-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>

<span class="figure-article-caption-owner"><p><a href="https://www.instagram.com/jonnymarlow" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="externalLink" data-ordinal="1" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Jonny Marlow</a></p></span>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_16-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Hathaway wouldn't change a thing. “We love going to sports games,” she says of spending time with her boys. “I just enjoy wearing jeans, playing pickup basketball and going to see the Knicks.”
</p>

<p id="mntl-sc-block_20-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> Later in the interview, Hathaway gets emotional talking about <a href="https://people.com/parents/anne-hathaway-pregnant-infertility-sisterhood-second-child/" data-component="link" data-source="inlineLink" data-type="internalLink" data-ordinal="1">her pregnancies.</a> "I just feel very lucky," she says. "I know that not everybody who wants to be a parent gets to be. I’m just blown away by how fortunate I am. It went really well for me twice, and that’s really lucky."
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_21-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>
<figure id="mntl-sc-block_22-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block people-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image mntl-sc-block-image--no-theme no-theme figure-landscape figure-high-res"> <div class="figure-media">

<p id="mntl-sc-block_23-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-html"> When asked if she could pinpoint the most beautiful part about being a mother, she hesitates for a minute: "I couldn't imagine identifying a single most. Being a mom is just this beautiful ongoing poem that I get to live inside of."
</p>
<div id="mntl-sc-block_24-0" class="comp mntl-sc-block mntl-sc-block-adslot mntl-block"></div>

gold_text:
The 'Devil Wears Prada 2' actress, who is on the cover of PEOPLE's World's Most Beautiful issue, shares two sons with husband Adam Shulman
Anne Hathaway has to be ready to play basketball at a moment's notice.

The actress — who shares two sons Jonathan, 10, and Jack, 6, with husband Adam Shulman — tells PEOPLE in her cover story for World's Most Beautiful 2026 about her favorite parts about being a "boy mom."

"I’m a tomboy! I love that being a boy mom has really informed my fashion," she says in the issue on newsstands Friday. "I have to always be able to go from a serious business meeting to, like, a basketball court. You have to be able to play pickup basketball at any point, in any outfit. So [I tell myself]: When you get dressed in the morning, dress wisely because you 100 percent are going to have a projectile thrown at you probably by the end of breakfast."
The Devil Wears Prada 2 star laughed with PEOPLE GM and Editor in Chief Charlotte Triggs about what it's like to be a boy mom like herself vs. a girl mom like Triggs (who has three daughters).
"Did you ever see the video about boy moms vs. girl moms? Oh my gosh. It's so funny," says Hathaway, 43. "It's like girl moms sit and they're like, coloring with their daughter and they're like, 'What a beautiful piece of art?' And it's like, so quiet. Boy moms, they're like, 'Where's your weapons' basket?'" she jokes.
Hathaway wouldn't change a thing. “We love going to sports games,” she says of spending time with her boys. “I just enjoy wearing jeans, playing pickup basketball and going to see the Knicks.”
Later in the interview, Hathaway gets emotional talking about her pregnancies. "I just feel very lucky," she says. "I know that not everybody who wants to be a parent gets to be. I’m just blown away by how fortunate I am. It went really well for me twice, and that’s really lucky."
When asked if she could pinpoint the most beautiful part about being a mother, she hesitates for a minute: "I couldn't imagine identifying a single most. Being a mom is just this beautiful ongoing poem that I get to live inside of."
